purpose: Define how postulation card data is sourced dynamically from HR, project config, manual config or computed rules, and what is visible to shoppers vs internal roles.
principle: All operational information must be live/dynamic and configurable. Nothing should be hard-coded. HR-derived fields may change and must reflect the current source of truth or latest safe preview.

scenarioSummary.notes: Scenario often comes from HR and may vary by visit.

availableFrom.notes: For Cinepolis this may depend on prior visit timing and can change dynamically.

allowedScheduleWindow.notes: Derived from availableFrom, franja, quincena, days and time rules when configured.

franjaOrDays.notes: Visible as user-friendly restriction only when it affects shopper scheduling.

measurementPeriod.notes: Do not show internal measurement labels unless needed for shopper action, such as Q1/Q2 selection.

restrictionSourceType.notes: Client requirement vs internal measurement is internal metadata. Shopper should see clear instruction, not internal taxonomy.

certificationRequirementSummary.notes: Show summary and link/CTA to certification when required.

documentsRequirementSummary.notes: Full resources/instructive may unlock after approval depending on project.

questionnaireMode.notes: Shopper only needs the correct action/button when questionnaire is available.

dynamicMappingRules: Every visible card field must declare whether it comes from HR mapping, project config, computed rule, admin override or fallback.
If HR source changes, card preview must use latest safe mapped value when backend/runtime is enabled later.
If mapping confidence is low, route to admin review/manual mapping, not hard-coded fallback.
Project creation wizard must allow manual configuration and later mapping/iteration.
Fields not relevant to shopper action must remain internal even if used for validation.

newLessons: Which card fields come from HR
Which card fields come from project configuration
Why internal restriction source is not shown to shopper
How postulation approval removes duplicate work

hardStops: Do not hard-code HR-derived values into code.
Do not show internal restriction source labels to shoppers unless intentionally configured for user-facing explanation.
Do not expose raw HR column names to shoppers.
Do not promise HR sync when sync gate is off.
Do not write Firestore from this contract.
